extra_model_fields.time_estimate: 2-4 hours (depending on rework complexity and producing CSV/text fallbacks)
verifier_recommended_fixes: Resolve any internal grader/verifier contradictions (not the worker's responsibility) but ensure submission artifacts are unambiguous and parseable.
If workbook uses features that may not parse in the grader environment, include plain-text formula excerpts and CSV exports of computed tables.

priority_items: {'rubric_item_id': 'ri_1', 'reason': 'Missing required deliverable (integrity_action_memo.md) and workbook not parseable for formula-level inspection; rubric requires both artifacts and visible logic.'}

extra_model_fields.time_estimate: 2-4 hours (depending on rework complexity and producing CSV/text fallbacks)
verifier_recommended_fixes: Resolve any internal grader/verifier contradictions (not the worker's responsibility) but ensure submission artifacts are unambiguous and parseable.
If workbook uses features that may not parse in the grader environment, include plain-text formula excerpts and CSV exports of computed tables.

planned_changes: Create integrity_action_memo.md (exact filename) that documents per-feature row-level citations to packet fields, decision rationale for each feature (pass/warn/fail/needs_review), HCA-specific notes, uncertainty/assumption section, and a final decision table.

Regenerate ili_dig_priority.xlsx ensuring it is saved as a standard .xlsx without macros or external add-ins; include the following sheets with visible formulas: Summary, Inputs, Model, Sensitivity, Checks.
Inputs sheet: reproduce the embedded packet exactly (one row per feature) and include a unique feature ID column used in all traceable citations.

Model sheet: implement rule logic in explicit Excel formulas (immediate rule: depth_pct_wall > 50 OR (HCA == 'Y' AND MAOP_pct > 85)); calculate MAOP risk scores and show the 20% pressure-reduction scenario lowering MAOP risk by 25% with intermediate calculation cells visible.
Sensitivity sheet: run +/- threshold scenarios for key thresholds and show impacts on decisions and scheduling.

Checks sheet: include automated validation rows that assert inputs equal packet values, that no external data is referenced, and that weekly dig assignments sum correctly and do not exceed dig_capacity_week = 2.
Scheduling: add a week-by-week prioritized dig schedule in Summary or a dedicated sheet that respects dig_capacity_week = 2 and documents tie-breaking logic.

Agent trace: produce a complete agent_trace.json following the AgentTrace schema capturing each action (read inputs, create/edit artifacts, run validations, errors handled). Also produce a normalized actual_outputs.json referencing final artifacts.

Add plain-text/CSV fallbacks: export key workbook sheets (Inputs, Model results, Summary, Sensitivity, Checks) as CSV or include a text file listing formulas and key computed columns to ensure reviewers can inspect calculations if openpyxl is unavailable.

Run a self-check before submission: open the .xlsx with an open-source parser (if possible) or verify CSV fallbacks parse correctly; confirm integrity_action_memo.md is present and named exactly as required.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Restore deliverable completeness and provide parseable, inspectable workbook and missing memo so rubric criteria can be evaluated; also supply a valid trace and fallback text/CSV in case environment parsing fails.
